message:
🐛 Make console non-blocking when clusters are unreachable (#910)

- Add waitWithDeadline() helper that caps multi-cluster API responses
  at 5 seconds, returning whatever data responsive clusters provided
- ListClusters now returns cluster metadata from kubeconfig immediately,
  enriches with cached health only, and kicks off background refresh
- Add GetCachedHealth() to read health cache without network calls
- Replace all 23 blocking wg.Wait() calls across mcp, gitops, and SSE
  handlers with the deadline-bounded version
- Individual per-cluster timeouts (15-45s) are preserved for slow but
  reachable clusters; the deadline only caps total response time

Previously, 12 unreachable clusters × 45s timeout = every dashboard
frozen for minutes. Now pages load in <1 second.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
